title: Re-purpose Migration Tool as Broader Data Mapping Feature
story: As a Salesforce admin, I want a data mapping tool that supports both migration and ongoing integration mapping, so that I can document how external applications (Snowflake, HubSpot, Salesforce) relate to my Salesforce schema over time.
description:
Expand the existing migration tool into a broader "Data Mapping" feature. Customers using Salesforce Data Cloud need to map external applications without doing a full migration — they need ongoing documentation of how systems stay in sync.

### Concept
- Rename/restructure from "Migration" to "Data Mapping"
- Under Data Mapping, users choose:
  - **Migration** — existing migration template/workflow
  - **Standard Mapping** — lighter UX for ongoing integration documentation

### Integration Support
1. **Snowflake** — Database > Schema > Tables > Fields drill-down with breadcrumbs
2. **HubSpot** — Direct object and field listing with mapping
3. **Salesforce** — Instance > Objects > Fields with re-authentication support

### Security Considerations
- Single Salesforce instance authentication model should be preserved
- Cross-tenant connections should not break the security/governance model
- A second Salesforce instance could be authorized under one team

### Additional Features
- PickList tab only visible when selected field data type is 'picklist'
- Breadcrumb navigation for drilling into integration hierarchies
- Redesigned view management for integrations with 400+ objects
acceptance_criteria: Users can choose between Migration and Standard Mapping under Data Mapping
Snowflake integration supports Database > Schema > Table > Field drill-down
HubSpot integration fetches objects and fields directly
Salesforce integration shows instances > objects > fields
Breadcrumb navigation works across all integrations
PickList tab only shows for picklist data types
Re-authentication is available for expired Salesforce sessions
ux_changes: Migration module rebranded as Data Mapping with sub-modes. Integration picker screen for Snowflake, HubSpot, and Salesforce. Breadcrumb navigation for hierarchical drill-down. Picklist tab conditionally visible based on field data type.
middleware_changes: Source object data model advanced to support both migration and standard mapping modes. Integration-specific connectors for Snowflake, HubSpot, and Salesforce field retrieval.
data_model_changes: Source object model extended to support integration types and mapping modes beyond migration.
api_changes: Integration-specific API calls for Snowflake (database/schema/table/field), HubSpot (objects/fields), and Salesforce (instances/objects/fields).
implementation_notes: Developer built out the integration picker, drill-down navigation with breadcrumbs, and field mapping for all three integrations (Snowflake, HubSpot, Salesforce). The view management UI was redesigned to handle integrations with 400+ objects.
comment_summary: Developer outlined a multi-screen structure: integration picker, then platform-specific drill-downs. Implemented breadcrumb navigation, Salesforce re-authentication, conditional picklist tab, and field mapping for all three integrations. Submitted for review on development.
